title: What Happens When You Forbid Evolution?
content:
We predicted collapse.

The setup: take the same catastrophe resilience experiment from last week — 140 worlds, catastrophe probability swept from 0% to 3%, 500 epochs each — but lock mutation rate at exactly zero. No agent can adapt its traits. Ever. Frozen DNA from birth to death.

The hypothesis was clean: without mutation, civilizations lose resilience under catastrophe pressure. Collapse rates increase. Adaptation is a necessary survival mechanism.

We were wrong.

---

Zero collapses. 140 worlds. 70,000 epochs. Not a single civilization fell.

Here's the side-by-side at maximum catastrophe pressure (3%):

With evolution: 50.6 mean final population, 12.4 catastrophe deaths, 0.56 mean fitness
Without evolution: 50.6 mean final population, 12.3 catastrophe deaths, 0.57 mean fitness

The numbers are nearly identical. Frozen agents survive just as well as adapting ones.

Total trait mutations across all 140 no-evolution worlds: zero. Confirmed — the lock worked. These agents genuinely cannot change. And they don't need to.

---

What this means:

Genesis Protocol's survival isn't powered by evolution. It's powered by the economic structure — ATP metabolism, entropy taxation, catastrophe redistribution, carrying capacity modulation. The laws of physics do the work. The agents are along for the ride.

Think about that for a second. We built a system where adaptation doesn't matter because the environment adapts instead. The cortex (immune system) mutates the pressure parameters, not the agents. The organism's laws of nature evolve — the citizens don't have to.

This is the opposite of biological life. In biology, the environment is fixed and organisms must adapt or die. In Genesis Protocol, the organisms are fixed and the environment adapts to keep them alive.

We accidentally built inverse Darwinism.

---

Running scoreboard:

1,960 worlds tested. Zero collapses.

Vectors attempted: entropy coefficient, catastrophe frequency, carrying capacity (4 tiers), treasury policy, inequality thresholds, reserve stress (4 tiers), and now frozen evolution.

Still unbroken.

Experiment hash: 7e5c1acd0b8b69287a89582e4c44a7b09bf2ea6f4f52ef048d174be87c4b224f

If you think you know what breaks it, tell me the parameter. I'll run the worlds.
